title: conformance validate-workflow CI
purpose: G3.1 defines conformance validate workflow ci for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake.
scope: Owns `G3.1` only; siblings under `G3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §9 — Branch G — Verification & quality plane (anti-mistake)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#9-branch-g-verification-quality-plane-anti-mistake).
behavior_steps: On each pull request, GitHub Actions (or equivalent CI) runs `validate-workflow.py` and `headless-verify` as S0 gates—the same commands local pursuit uses from task cards and [F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) pack verify suites.
CI validates repo conformance and configured task evidence paths; it does not mutate `journal/state.json` or clear H1/H3 design gates in the pursuit state machine.
When a check fails, the workflow posts a comment linking to uploaded evidence logs so operators can triage without opening the IDE session.
Green CI is a prerequisite for merge and for downstream `goal_verify`, but passing checks alone does not mark a goal achieved or waive H3 sign-off.
If CI is misconfigured, unreachable, or returns indeterminate results, merge and goal-level verification fail closed at H2 until headless verify mirrors local `verify-router.py` behavior.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Flaky test â€” escalation S4 once, then H2 with evidence log; no silent retry loop.
Edge case `G3.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `G3.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Extend verify-router for goal-level suite invocation.
Wire CI: validate-workflow checks goal block when pursuit.mode=goal_autopilot.
Validate `G3.1` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `G3.1` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `G3.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: G3.1 defines conformance validate workflow ci for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake. In practice, the system on each pull request, github actions (or equivalent ci) runs `validate-workflow.py` and `headless-verify` as s0 gates—the same commands local pursuit uses from task cards and [f1.8](f1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) pack verify suites, then ci validates repo conformance and configured task evidence paths; it does not mutate `journal/state.json` or clear h1/h3 design gates in the pursuit state machine.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-g 2026-06-28 -->

`scripts/validate-workflow.py` is the conformance gate for journal/state shape, required fields, and pursuit invariants. CI and optional pre-push hooks run it so corrupt dual-writes fail before merge, not mid-autopilot.

validate-workflow complements verify-router: the former checks workflow schema and routing consistency; the latter runs product tests. Both are S0-first ([B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)). Failures block continue/autopilot until the conductor repairs state or records an operator exception.
